rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. ISSUES (to be addressed at hearing): 1) The purpose of the hearing is to determine why there has been no response to the Examiner's notes. 2) Examiner Notes: a. Please explain the starting date for the account. Page 1 does not state. Page 2 line 8 alleges it starts October 31, 2015. Page 5 line 6 alleges it begins November 1, 2015. The GC-400 alleges the account begins November 1, 2015. Property on Hand at start of account period alleges it begins October 31, 2015. b. Please explain and provide the legal basis for the judicial council form GC-400 property on hand at beginning of account not matching the prior account property on hand at the end of the account. c. Please explain and provide the legal basis for why First Rep. Account #2699 the ending balances of the prior account does not match the starting balances of this account. d. Please explain the legal costs reimbursement for 5/26/20216 on Schedule C - Disbursements. It appears $670.80 was paid to the attorney but receipts show only $573.30 in expenses. Attorney Vicken Sonentz appeared via courtcall for the 5/10/2016 hearing. Please explain why the estate should pay the costs of a roundtrip ticket and hotel reservation for the attorney when the attorney appeared via courtcall. e. Please explain the legal costs reimbursed for 10/21/2016 on Schedule C - Disbursements and file the missing receipts. $200.21 was alleged for lodging no receipt filed and counsel appeared via court call. Please explain why the estate should pay the cost of lodging when the attorney appeared via courtcall. It is alleged the court call was $116.00 but the provided court call confirmation page which is not a receipt alleged the statewide fee for court call appears is $86.00.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MATTHEW SHUI SANG MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/19/2022 at direction of the judicial officer. Examiner notes also emailed 6/10/2022 and requested confirmation from the attorney that they receieved the notes. 1) Please prove the estate is entirely community property of Decedent. 2) The account must be amended to only include this estate's assets and cannot be a combined account that also includes the related matter #303207. 3) The petition page 1 line 23 alleges Linda Yee Lina Ma died testate when the Decedent is Matthew Shui Sang Ma. 4) Decedent died 08/29/2016. Unclear when account begins as page 1 line 21 alleges the account begins 08/28/2016, page 4 line 2 alleges the account begins 08/28/2018, the summary of account alleges the beginning assets on hand on 08/28/2016 and Schedule A alleges beginning assets on hand 08/29/2016. A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. (See LRSF 14.34). 5) Petition must be signed and verified by Petitioner. All pleadings filed in proceedings under the Probate Code must be verified. If two or more persons join in a pleading, it may be verified by any of them. (See CRC 7.103). 6) The petition alleges the I&A value of $601,834.77 contains all the assets of Decedent's ancillary probate estate. Please file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of the account beginning with $1,203,669.54 as accounts must begin with the I&A values. 7) Please explain and file an itemized request for all alleged expenses owed to Executor. Please address Probate Code 9252. 8) Please explain on schedule D - Disbursements Appraisal of 300 Berry St., Unit 1108 for $350.00 and file a receipt. Please address Probate Code 8900 et seq and why this appraisal was necessary when the court appointed a probate referee. 9) Local Rule 14.57 B says mileage and parking are absorbed as part of fees. It appears the "travel fees" on disbursements totaling $1,747.25 must be paid back to the estate and a receipt filed. 10) The petition alleges the account period ends 08/28/2018. The 11/04/2021 Declaration has a bank statement from April 22, 2016 to May 20, 2016. Please verify the balance at the end of the account period. See LRSF 14.34 D. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VIRGINIA CAROLINE MARTINEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Reimbursements by the administrator and Delina Martinez were for their share of disbursements paid out of the estate and, therefore, not receipts includable in the fee base. 2) There was nothing attached to Delina Grace Martinez's late filed creditorâs claim to support her assertion that $19,910.44 represents just Decedent's 1/3 share of the home equity credit line taken in June 2012 (or 2013?). 3) The late filed creditor's claim by Daniel Myers was for work done on 10/1/20 -- after Decedent died. It appears that that $5,350.00 should be the responsibility of the remaining life estate holders. There was also no invoice or any supporting documentation attached to his claim. Administrator merely mentioned the Josephine Martinez Trust in her petition without identifying the real property and later, in her supplement filed on 5/25/22, provided the case number in which that testamentary trust was created. That case, PES-93-260361, predated scanning. Administrator has not provided a copy of the order establishing that testamentary trust. An amended judgment was filed on 10/9/12, which seems to be the (complete?) final order. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERNEST DARYL JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: An original statement for the annuity must be filed to verify the ending balance of that contract, accompanied by the administrator's or attorney's declaration re retention of the original or computer-generated printout. [LRSF 14.34 C. and CRC 7.575(b).] Is the attorney verifying that the amount shown as held in the attorney trust account is, in fact, the balance as of 12/9/21? The "cost retainer" received from Virginia Campbell (who?) may not be included in the fee base, as it was not a true receipt during estate administration but reimbursed out of the estate. A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] There is no satisfaction of claim attached to the petition, as alleged in paragraph 6. The filing fee for the initial petition was $450, not $480. If there were additional costs associated with the filing, they must be clearly itemized. Other "court fling fee" entries without any description of what was filed are inadequate. (Notice was given to the FTB before the appointment of the "general personal representative". There were real and personal property taxes due?) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/22/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/26/2022.Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Personal representative must give 15 days notice of the petition. Petitions need to include sufficient allegations to allow the Court to determine administration is proceeding properly. Please make all necessary allegations as if the petition was for final distribution, including but not limited to: Notice under Probate Code 9202; Creditor's Claims; Assignments/Powers of Attorney; Requests for Special Notice, etc. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/11/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH FLOYD DEROUEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/26/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The petition page 2 alleges all notices due of the hearing on the petition for final distribution of the Estate have been given in the manner prescribed by law. This does not appear to be accurate as notice has not been filed for this petition. Administrator must give notice of the petition for final distribution. (Probate Code 1202). 2) It appears Administrator JoAnn Jones paid her own creditor claim without following LRSF 14.29 and Probate Code 9252. Please explain and file legal authority for the administrator paying her own creditor claim and not following LRSF 14.29 and Probate Code 9252. The matter will not go forward until Administrator complies with LRSF 14.29 and Probate Code 9252. 3) Please provide an itemized list of all reimbursements sought and file receipts for the requested reimbursements. If no receipt is available please file a memoradum of points and authorities in support of the court allowing reimbursement with no receipt. 4) According to the petition page 3 lines 13-15 over $250,000.00 has been kept in a single account when the FDIC limit is $250,000.00. Please explain why the administrator appears to have breached their duty and kept over $250,000.00 in a single account when the FDIC limit is $250,000.00. 5) The caption of the order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.35 I. 6) b.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/11/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/26/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34 D.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/11/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WOON FUNG CHAN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions And Confirming Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/22. There is no legal description that is supposed to be Exhibit A attached to this third amended petition. This third amended petition still does not specify that only Woon Fung Chan's 50% interest is to be confirmed to her trust. This was only alleged by the attorney in a supplement filed on 2/24/22, for which no notice was given. (The footer of the third amended pleading shows "Second Amended Petition," etc.) Proof of service filed on 5/24/22 (no courtesy copy) shows that notice was mailed on 5/24/22 for a hearing on 6/21/22. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (verified supplement to the third amended petition, providing the missing legal description, correctly identifying the property interest at issue, and proof of service of the notice of the third amended petition and any supplement or amendment thereto) must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 7/22/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/26/2022. Court calendar full. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Chunlin Li has filed a disclaimer. Keaton on the back of page 1. 4) Please provide more information regarding how the court can determine community property funds were exclusively used to purchase the property. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/11/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHIRLEY S.M. CHONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/27/2022. Court calendar full. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Same issues as prior tentative: 1) Petitioner William Chong must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) Petitioner William Chong must file proof of service of the summons. 3) Petitioner William Chong please file a complete copy of the 2016 trust and any and all amendments. 4) Petitioner Xue Jun Chong must file proof of service of the summons. 5) Petitioner Xue Jun Chong please give 15 days' notice to all beneficiaries named in the 1991 trust. (Probate Code 1202). 6) Petitioner Xue Jun Chong please file a complete copy of the 1991 trust and any and all amendments. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/11/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SYLVIA WALKER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Order Authorizing Particular Transactions
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/02/2022 by the assigned judicial officer after review of the file. The petitioner's estate planning described in paragraph 3 of the supplement filed on 06/14/2022 must be presented as an amendment to the petition. A new citation must be issued and timely served with a copy of the amendment on the respondent spouse. Counsel for the petitioner and respondent spouse must meet and confer and provide copies of the proposed "revocable" trust and other estate planning documents affecting the respondent spouse's interest in the assets sought to be transmuted to the petitioner as his sole and separate property.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF QUEENIE ANDERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/26/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C. 3)] 4) No date of death given for Donald Anderson. The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D. 1)] 5) If an heir or devisee died after the decedent, the petitioner must allege whether or not a personal representative has been appointed for such deceased heir or devisee. If no personal representative has been appointed for the post-deceased heir or devisee, or such personal representative is the same person as the petitioner, all heirs and/or devisees of the post-deceased heir or devisee must be listed. [LRSF 14.18 D. 3)] 6) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 7) Petitioner must file proof of publication. [Probate Code 8120 et seq] 8) Petitioner must file Notice of Petition to Administer Estate DE-121. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/11/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
